The fourteenth annual conven.
ton of the State Dairymen's asso-
citation ended last evening with
the election of the following
officers:


President, D. S. Troy; vice presi-
dent, E. ]. Ross; secretary and
treasurer, Ira P. Whitney; direc-
tors, David Brown, J. T. Durdle, A.
M. Stevens, l.. S. Thomle.


The association suggested Stan
wood, Wash, as the meeting place
of the next convention and that
city will be formally selected by
the directors. Next years gather
ing will be held earlier in Deem
Der in order that the dairymen may
be able to also attend the State
Livestock associations convention.


Among the resolutions passed
before adjournment was one call
ing for a state appropriation of
$4,000 for farmers Institutes in
the next two years. A livestock
and dairy exhibit was urged for the
Alaska-Yukon-Pacifc exposition at
Seattle, and John l.. Smith, of Spo-
kane, endorsed as superintendent
of these exhibits.

ton needed by the dairy interests
and to the report of L. Davies,
dairy and food commissioner. Mr..
Davies urged systematic milk in
spection for cities Of the first
class, the city inspectors to be
connected with the state depart.
ment as specials.
